audienceSections.normal: This chapter teaches [[grammar:clause + terwijl + clause]] and [[grammar:adjective-er + dan]]. Use [[grammar:terwijl]] when two things happen at the same time. Add [[grammar:-er]] to many adjectives and use [[grammar:dan]] for the second item.
audienceSections.expert: This chapter introduces [[grammar:clause + terwijl + clause]]: [[grammar:Terwijl]] introduces a subordinate clause with verb-final order and can carry temporal or contrastive force.; [[grammar:adjective-er + dan]]: The comparative degree is marked morphologically and the comparison standard is introduced by [[grammar:dan]].

audienceSections.normal:
#### clause + terwijl + clause

Use [[grammar:terwijl]] when two things happen at the same time. Example: [[grammar:De trein rijdt snel, terwijl de tram vaak stopt.]]

#### adjective-er + dan

Add [[grammar:-er]] to many adjectives and use [[grammar:dan]] for the second item. Example: [[grammar:De trein is sneller dan de tram.]]
audienceSections.expert:
#### clause + terwijl + clause

[[grammar:Terwijl]] introduces a subordinate clause with verb-final order and can carry temporal or contrastive force. Literal example: [[grammar:De trein rijdt snel, terwijl de tram vaak stopt.]]

#### adjective-er + dan

The comparative degree is marked morphologically and the comparison standard is introduced by [[grammar:dan]]. Literal example: [[grammar:De trein is sneller dan de tram.]]

audienceSections.normal: [[grammar:Terwijl]] connects simultaneous situations. The comparative usually takes [[grammar:-er]] and introduces the standard with [[grammar:dan]].
audienceSections.expert: [[grammar:Terwijl]] connects simultaneous situations. The comparative usually takes [[grammar:-er]] and introduces the standard with [[grammar:dan]]. The grammar IDs remain distinct and cumulative.

breakdown.normal:
- [[grammar:Welke route nemen we naar het concert?]] — Which route shall we take to the concert?
- [[grammar:De trein is sneller dan de tram.]] — The train is faster than the tram.
- [[grammar:Ja, maar bij de trein moet je overstappen.]] — Yes, but with the train you have to transfer.
- [[grammar:De tram rijdt rechtstreeks naar het centrum.]] — The tram goes directly to the city center.
- [[grammar:De trein rijdt snel, terwijl de tram vaak stopt.]] — The train goes fast, while the tram stops often.
- [[grammar:De tram is langzamer dan de trein.]] — The tram is slower than the train.
- [[grammar:Welke route is goedkoper?]] — Which route is cheaper?
- [[grammar:De tramkaart is goedkoper, maar de reis duurt langer.]] — The tram ticket is cheaper, but the journey takes longer.
- [[grammar:Ik lees terwijl we in de tram zitten.]] — I read while we are sitting on the tram.
- [[grammar:Dan kiezen we de rustige route.]] — Then we will choose the calm route.
- [[grammar:Goed, we vertrekken om zeven uur.]] — Good, we will leave at seven o’clock.
- [[grammar:Dan zijn we ruim op tijd.]] — Then we will be there well on time.
